activities_projects.title: Character Feelings Chart
activities_projects.objective: Understand the emotions of the young seagull and the pilot.
steps: List the main characters: young seagull, the pilot
Write down their feelings in different situations
Share findings in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will recognize character emotions and relate to the text.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of emotions and clarity

activities_projects.title: Draw the Flight Scene
activities_projects.objective: Visualize the characters’ first flying experience.
steps: Read the story carefully
Draw the young seagull taking its first flight or the pilot flying over mountains
Label key actions or emotions in the drawing
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will improve visualization and comprehension skills.

activities_projects.title: Simple Timeline of Events
activities_projects.objective: Understand the sequence of events in both stories.
steps: Write 5 main events for each story in order
Draw a simple timeline for visual clarity
Discuss with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will learn to organize events chronologically.

activities_projects.title: Diary Entry – Young Seagull
steps: Imagine being the young seagull before first flight
Write a short diary entry expressing feelings of fear and excitement
Share with a partner or class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop empathy and creative expression.
activities_projects.evaluation: Emotional depth and connection to the story

activities_projects.title: Word Meaning List
activities_projects.objective: Learn new vocabulary from the stories.
steps: Pick 8 new words from both stories
Write their meanings
Use each word in a simple sentence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance vocabulary and sentence-making skills.

activities_projects.title: Role Play – First Flight
activities_projects.objective: Act out key scenes to understand actions and emotions.
steps: Choose a scene from either story
Assign roles (young seagull, parent, pilot, observer)
Perform in class showing expressions and movements
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will improve expressive skills and comprehension.

activities_projects.title: Theme Poster
activities_projects.objective: Identify main theme like courage and overcoming fear.
steps: Write the theme of each story on a poster
Add one example from the story
Decorate with illustrations related to flying
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will recognize and present key themes.

activities_projects.title: Short Speech – Overcoming Fear
activities_projects.objective: Relate story lessons to real life.
steps: Prepare a 1-minute speech on overcoming fear using examples from the stories
Present confidently in class
Answer one question from classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop speaking skills and personal connection to literature.

activities_projects.title: Illustrate the Pilot’s Journey
activities_projects.objective: Visual comprehension of the story setting.
steps: Draw the pilot flying through mountains or fog
Include details from the text to make the scene accurate
Share with classmates and explain the drawing
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance imagination and attention to detail.

activities_projects.title: Simple Quiz Preparation
activities_projects.objective: Reinforce understanding of events and characters.
steps: Prepare 5 simple questions from each story
Pair up and ask each other questions
Check answers with the text
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will revise the story and enhance recall skills.
